name: RStudio On Slurm
summary: Launch policy-aware RStudio or Posit sessions from Slurm allocations.
description: This skill provides a conservative pattern for RStudio or Posit IDE sessions on Slurm-backed HPC systems, including policy checks, project-directory review, optional tunnel guidance, and plan-only defaults before launching a site-approved wrapper.

tools.name: sbatch
tools.notes: Used by the example batch wrapper for short interactive IDE allocations.

tools.name: R
tools.notes: R must be available in the session environment selected by the site.

tools.name: rserver
tools.notes: Some sites expose a user-safe RStudio Server wrapper; many production sites use Posit Workbench Launcher instead.

tools.name: ssh
tools.notes: Used only for sites that require an SSH tunnel to reach the compute-node web session.

inputs.name: project_dir
inputs.description: R project or working directory to open inside the scheduled session.

inputs.name: port
inputs.description: Local web port used by a site-approved RStudio wrapper when direct Workbench access is not available.

inputs.name: launch_command
inputs.description: Site-approved RStudio, Posit Workbench, Open OnDemand, or wrapper command for starting the IDE session.

inputs.name: site_policy
inputs.description: Local policy confirming whether user-started RStudio sessions and tunnels are allowed on compute nodes.

outputs.name: rstudio_session_log
outputs.description: Slurm output showing compute host, project directory, selected port, and wrapper command review.

outputs.name: connection_notes
outputs.description: SSH tunnel or Workbench connection notes for reaching the scheduled IDE session.

examples.title: Plan or launch a site-approved RStudio session on Slurm
Check RStudio, R, Slurm, and project prerequisites
Build an SSH tunnel command when required by the site
Review site policy before starting an RStudio session

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
After site approval, run the preflight, submit a short plan-only job, then set RUN_RSTUDIO_SESSION=1 with a site-approved launch command and cancel the job when finished.
